sentiment.rationale: Contrasts the blessed man who delights in the law with the wicked who perish, establishing a foundational wisdom framework.
A royal psalm proclaiming the Lord's anointed king over the nations, with a warning to the rebellious.
David's trust in God as his shield while fleeing Absalom, expressing confidence amid distress.
An evening prayer of trust, calling others to put confidence in the Lord who grants peace.
A morning prayer asking God to lead in righteousness and to judge the wicked and deceitful.
A penitential psalm of deep anguish, weeping, and pleading for mercy in the face of divine chastening.
David appeals to God as righteous judge to vindicate him and bring justice upon the wicked.
A hymn of wonder at God's majesty in creation and the dignity he bestows upon humanity.
Praise for God's righteous judgments upon the nations, with a plea to remember the afflicted.
A lament over the arrogance of the wicked who oppress the poor, calling on God to arise.
Declares trust in the Lord when the foundations are destroyed; God tests and judges.
Laments the disappearance of the faithful and the prevalence of flattering and lying tongues.
A heartfelt cry of 'How long?' expressing feelings of abandonment, yet ending in trust.
Declares that the fool says there is no God; all have gone astray and become corrupt.
Describes the character of one who may dwell in God's holy hill: integrity, truth, and justice.
A messianic psalm of confident trust; fullness of joy and pleasures forevermore at God's right hand.
David pleads for vindication, asking God to keep him as the apple of his eye from the wicked.
A triumphant song of deliverance after victory; God is rock, fortress, shield, and horn of salvation.
The heavens declare God's glory; the law of the Lord is perfect, converting the soul.
A prayer for the king's victory, trusting in the name of the Lord rather than chariots.
Celebrates the king's joy in God's strength and the blessings of answered prayer.
The messianic psalm of suffering beginning 'My God, why hast thou forsaken me,' ending in praise.
The beloved shepherd psalm expressing complete trust, provision, and comfort in the Lord's care.
A processional psalm celebrating the King of glory entering his gates in triumph.
An acrostic prayer for guidance, forgiveness, and deliverance, remembering God's tender mercies.
A plea of integrity, asking God to examine and try the psalmist who walks in truth.
Expresses confidence that the Lord is light and salvation; one thing desired: to dwell in God's house.
A cry for help followed by praise when God hears, asking judgment on the deceitful.
A psalm of divine power; the voice of the Lord thunders over the waters, breaks cedars, shakes the wilderness.
A thanksgiving for healing; weeping may endure for a night but joy cometh in the morning.
A prayer of trust in deep distress; into thy hand I commit my spirit.
Blessed is the one whose transgression is forgiven; confession brings relief and divine guidance.
A hymn of praise for God's creative word, faithfulness, and watchful eye over those who hope in him.
Praise and instruction: taste and see that the Lord is good; the Lord delivers the righteous.
An imprecatory prayer for God to fight against persecutors and vindicate the innocent.
Contrasts the wickedness of the transgressor with the lovingkindness of God that reaches the heavens.
Wisdom instruction: fret not because of evildoers; trust in the Lord and do good.
A penitential psalm of deep suffering under the weight of sin, pleading for God not to forsake.
A meditation on the brevity and vanity of life, asking God to hear the prayer of a stranger on earth.
Thanksgiving for deliverance; obedience is preferred over sacrifice; ends with a plea for help.
Blessed is the one who considers the poor; betrayal by a close friend, yet God upholds.
The soul thirsts for God like a deer for water; asks why art thou cast down, O my soul.
A plea for God to send light and truth; echoes the refrain of hope from Psalm 42.
A national lament: God has cast off his people despite their faithfulness; a cry for help.
A royal wedding psalm celebrating the king's splendor and the bride's beauty; messianic overtones.
God is our refuge and strength; be still and know that I am God.
A jubilant psalm calling all nations to clap hands and shout; God reigns over the nations.
Celebrates Mount Zion as the city of the great King, beautiful for situation, the joy of the whole earth.
Wisdom meditation on the futility of wealth; none can redeem a brother or buy off death.
God speaks as judge, rebuking empty ritual and calling for sincere thanksgiving and obedience.
David's great penitential psalm after his sin: create in me a clean heart, O God.
Denounces the deceitful tongue of the mighty man; the righteous will trust in God's mercy.
Restatement of Psalm 14: the fool has said in his heart there is no God.
A short prayer for deliverance when the Ziphims betrayed David; ends in confident praise.
Lament over betrayal by a close companion; cast thy burden upon the Lord and he shall sustain thee.
Trust amid fear when enemies press; in God I will praise his word; God records every tear.
In the cave, David sings: my heart is fixed, O God; thy mercy is great unto the heavens.
An imprecatory psalm denouncing unjust judges and calling for God's swift justice.
Prayer for deliverance from enemies who lie in wait; God is my defense and refuge.
A national lament after military defeat, pleading for God's help: give us help from trouble.
A prayer from the end of the earth: lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
Quiet confidence: my soul waiteth upon God; he only is my rock and salvation.
Longing for God in the wilderness; thy lovingkindness is better than life.
Prayer against the secret plots of the wicked; God turns their own arrows against them.
A harvest thanksgiving celebrating God's provision, the abundance of rain, and the fruitful earth.
A communal hymn of praise for God's awesome works and personal testimony of answered prayer.
A missionary psalm asking God to bless so that all nations may know his saving power.
A triumphant processional psalm celebrating God's march through history; he gives strength to his people.
A messianic psalm of intense suffering and zeal for God's house; imprecations against persecutors.
A brief urgent cry for swift divine help, nearly identical to the end of Psalm 40.
An elderly person's prayer for continued faithfulness, recounting God's lifelong care and asking not to be cast off in old age.
A messianic/royal psalm envisioning the ideal king who brings justice, peace, and universal dominion.
Asaph struggles with the prosperity of the wicked until he enters God's sanctuary and sees their end.
A communal lament over the destruction of the temple, asking God to remember his covenant.
God declares he will judge uprightly; the horn of the wicked will be cut off.
Celebrates God's fearsome power in defeating enemies; he is glorious and excellent from Zion.
A night of spiritual anguish, questioning if God has forgotten mercy, then remembering his ancient wonders.
A long historical psalm recounting Israel's repeated rebellion and God's patient but just responses.
A lament over the destruction of Jerusalem, pleading for mercy and deliverance from the nations.
A plea for restoration using the vine metaphor; cause thy face to shine and we shall be saved.
A festival psalm where God speaks, lamenting Israel's stubbornness and the blessings they forfeited.
God stands in the divine council judging unjust rulers; ye are gods but shall die like men.
A prayer against a coalition of enemy nations plotting to destroy Israel, invoking past divine victories.
Longing for the courts of the Lord; a day in thy courts is better than a thousand elsewhere.
Prayer for national revival; mercy and truth are met together, righteousness and peace have kissed.
A personal prayer of David: incline thine ear, for I am poor and needy; thou art great and full of compassion.
A Zion psalm celebrating the city of God; all nations find their spiritual birth there.
The darkest psalm in the Psalter; unrelieved suffering with no resolution, ending in darkness.
Celebrates the Davidic covenant and God's faithfulness, then laments the apparent rejection of the anointed.
Moses' prayer on human frailty and God's eternity; teach us to number our days.
A psalm of divine protection: he that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under his shadow.
A Sabbath song of praise for God's works; the righteous flourish like the palm tree.
The Lord reigneth; he is clothed with majesty and strength; his throne is established of old.
Calls on God to judge the arrogant oppressors; blessed is the man whom God chastens.
A call to worship and warning: today if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts.
A new song to the Lord; declare his glory among the nations; he cometh to judge the earth.
The Lord reigneth with fire and righteousness; light is sown for the righteous.
A new song for the Lord's marvelous works; all creation rejoices before him as judge.
The Lord reigns in holiness; he answered Moses, Aaron, and Samuel and forgave their people.
A universal call to joyful worship: know that the Lord he is God; his mercy is everlasting.
A royal pledge of integrity and justice; David vows to walk within his house with a perfect heart.
A prayer of the afflicted; compares fleeting life to grass while God's years endure forever.
Bless the Lord, O my soul; God forgives, heals, redeems, crowns with lovingkindness, and is merciful.
A magnificent creation hymn celebrating God's wisdom in ordering the natural world.
Recounts God's faithful dealings from Abraham through the Exodus, remembering his covenant.
A historical confession of Israel's recurring sins and God's merciful deliverance despite their rebellion.
Four pictures of distress and deliverance; O that men would praise the Lord for his goodness.
David's heart is fixed to praise God among the nations; a prayer combining parts of Psalms 57 and 60.
A strong imprecatory psalm against a treacherous accuser, ending with a plea for divine help.
A messianic psalm: sit at my right hand; thou art a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.
An acrostic hymn of praise for God's works; the fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.
An acrostic psalm on the blessings of the righteous who fear the Lord and delight in his commandments.
Praise the Lord who is high above all nations yet stoops to raise the poor from the dust.
A short, vivid celebration of the Exodus: the sea fled, Jordan turned back, mountains skipped.
Contrasts the living God with lifeless idols; trust in the Lord who blesses those who fear him.
Personal thanksgiving for deliverance from death; precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints.
The shortest psalm and chapter in the Bible; a universal call to praise God for his truth and mercy.
A processional thanksgiving: the stone the builders refused is become the head corner; this is the day the Lord has made.
The great acrostic meditation on the law: 176 verses celebrating the word of God in every facet.
A song of ascents lamenting dwelling among those who hate peace and speak deceitfully.
A song of ascents: my help cometh from the Lord; he that keepeth thee will not slumber.
A song of ascents rejoicing at going to the house of the Lord; pray for the peace of Jerusalem.
A song of ascents: eyes lifted to God for mercy amid the contempt of the proud.
A song of ascents: if the Lord had not been on our side, the flood would have overwhelmed us.
A song of ascents: those who trust in the Lord are like Mount Zion, which cannot be removed.
A song of ascents: when the Lord turned again the captivity of Zion; they that sow in tears shall reap in joy.
A song of ascents: except the Lord build the house, they labour in vain; children are a heritage.
A song of ascents: blessed is everyone that feareth the Lord; thy wife as a fruitful vine.
A song of ascents: Israel recounts affliction from youth; a curse upon those who hate Zion.
A song of ascents: out of the depths have I cried; with the Lord there is mercy and plenteous redemption.
A song of ascents: a brief expression of childlike humility and quiet trust in the Lord.
A song of ascents recalling David's vow and God's covenant promise to establish Zion and David's throne.
A song of ascents: how good and pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity.
The final song of ascents: a brief call to night-time worship and a blessing from Zion.
Praise the Lord for his goodness, sovereignty, and mighty deeds; contrasts with lifeless idols.
The Great Hallel with the refrain 'for his mercy endureth forever' repeated in every verse.
By the rivers of Babylon: a lament of exile with a fierce closing imprecation against the captors.
David praises God wholeheartedly; the Lord regards the lowly but knows the proud afar off.
A profound meditation on God's omniscience and omnipresence; fearfully and wonderfully made.
Prayer for deliverance from violent and deceitful men; confidence that God upholds the cause of the afflicted.
An evening prayer asking God to set a watch before the mouth and keep from the snares of the wicked.
A prayer from the cave: no one cares for my soul; thou art my refuge and my portion.
A penitential psalm: enter not into judgment with thy servant; teach me to do thy will.
A royal psalm blending praise for God as rock and fortress with a prayer for national prosperity.
David's acrostic hymn of praise: the Lord is gracious, full of compassion, and great beyond searching out.
Praise the Lord: trust not in princes but in God who keeps truth forever and executes justice.
Praise for God who heals the brokenhearted, counts the stars, sends snow, and gives his word to Israel.
A cosmic hymn calling all creation—angels, sun, moon, stars, mountains, beasts—to praise the Lord.
A new song to the Lord with dancing and instruments; the saints execute judgment upon the nations.
The grand doxology closing the Psalter: praise God with every instrument; let everything that hath breath praise the Lord.

summaries.summary: Contrasts the blessed man who delights in the law of the Lord with the wicked who are like chaff driven by the wind.
The nations rage against the Lord and his anointed, but God declares his king set upon Zion and promises him dominion over the earth.
David expresses confidence in God as his shield and sustainer while fleeing from his son Absalom's rebellion.
An evening prayer of trust in which David calls upon God, rebukes the faithless, and lies down in peace.
A morning prayer asking God to lead in righteousness, destroy the deceitful, and bless the righteous who take refuge in him.
A penitential cry from deep suffering, pleading with the Lord not to rebuke in anger, with tears and groaning for mercy.
David appeals to God as righteous judge to save him from persecution and bring justice upon the wicked.
Praises the majesty of God displayed in creation and the honor bestowed upon humanity as stewards of the earth.
David praises God for righteous judgments against the nations and calls upon him to remember the afflicted.
A lament over the arrogance of the wicked who oppress the helpless, calling on God to arise and defend the fatherless.
David declares his trust in the Lord when the foundations are destroyed; the Lord tests the righteous and judges the wicked.
A plea for help amid widespread deceit and flattery, with God's promise that his pure words will preserve the faithful.
David cries out 'How long?' in anguish at feeling forgotten, then resolves to trust in God's mercy and sing.
Declares that the fool says there is no God; all have become corrupt, but the Lord is the refuge of his people.
Describes the moral character required to dwell in God's presence: integrity, truthfulness, and justice.
A messianic psalm of trust: God is the source of all good, and at his right hand are pleasures forevermore.
David pleads for vindication, asking God to keep him as the apple of his eye and hide him under the shadow of his wings.
A grand song of deliverance celebrating God as rock, fortress, and deliverer after David's victories over his enemies.
The heavens declare God's glory and the law of the Lord is perfect; a prayer for cleansing from secret faults.
A prayer for the king before battle, trusting in the name of the Lord rather than chariots and horses.
Thanksgiving for the king's blessings: God has given him his heart's desire and crowned him with glory.
Begins with the cry of abandonment on the cross, describes intense suffering, and ends in worldwide praise and worship.
The Lord is my shepherd; a psalm of complete trust describing God's provision, guidance, comfort, and eternal goodness.
A processional psalm declaring the earth is the Lord's and calling for the gates to open for the King of glory.
An acrostic prayer for guidance and forgiveness, asking God to teach his paths and remember his tender mercies.
David pleads his integrity before God, asking to be examined and tried, and declares his love for God's house.
Expresses confident trust that the Lord is light and salvation; seeks one thing: to dwell in God's house forever.
A cry for help to God as rock and shield, praising him when the prayer is heard, and blessing his people.
Ascribes glory to God whose voice thunders over the waters, shatters cedars, shakes the wilderness, and gives strength.
Thanksgiving for healing and deliverance from death; weeping may endure for a night, but joy comes in the morning.
A prayer of trust in distress, committing the spirit into God's hands, and praising his wonderful kindness.
Blessed is the one whose sin is forgiven; confession brings relief, and God surrounds the trusting with mercy.
A hymn praising God's creative word, faithful counsel, and watchful eye over all who hope in his mercy.
An acrostic psalm of praise: taste and see that the Lord is good; the Lord delivers the righteous from all troubles.
David pleads with God to fight against those who fight against him and to vindicate his innocence.
Contrasts the wickedness of the transgressor with the infinite lovingkindness, faithfulness, and light of God.
Wisdom teaching: fret not because of evildoers; trust in the Lord, delight in him, and he shall give the desires of thy heart.
A penitential psalm of suffering under the burden of sin, forsaken by friends, pleading for God's presence.
Reflects on the brevity and vanity of human life, asking God to hear the prayer of a transient stranger.
Thanksgiving for being lifted from the miry pit; God delights in obedience more than sacrifice; a plea for help.
Blessed is the one who considers the poor; David is betrayed by a close friend but trusts in God's upholding.
The soul thirsts for God like a deer pants for water; despite despair, hope in God is the refrain.
A plea for God to send his light and truth to lead to the holy hill; echoes the hopeful refrain of Psalm 42.
A national lament: God has rejected his people despite their faithfulness; a cry for deliverance for his mercy's sake.
A royal wedding song celebrating the king's beauty, righteousness, and might, and the bride's splendor.
God is our refuge and strength; though the earth be removed, the Lord of hosts is with us; be still and know.
A jubilant call for all nations to clap hands and shout to God, who reigns as king over all the earth.
Celebrates the greatness of God in Zion, the joy of the whole earth, and his everlasting protection.
A wisdom psalm on the futility of wealth: none can redeem a brother from death, for man in honor abides not.
God speaks as judge, calling for genuine worship and thanksgiving rather than empty ritual sacrifice.
David's prayer of repentance after his sin: wash me, create in me a clean heart, and restore the joy of salvation.
Denounces the deceitful boasting of the wicked; the righteous trust in God's mercy like a green olive tree.
A restatement of Psalm 14: the fool says there is no God, and all have gone astray.
David prays for deliverance when the Ziphims reveal his hiding place; God is his helper and sustainer.
A lament over betrayal by a trusted companion; cast thy burden upon the Lord and he shall sustain thee.
David trusts God amid fear when enemies pursue him; God records his tears; in God I will praise his word.
From a cave, David's heart is fixed on God; he sings of mercy reaching to the heavens and truth to the clouds.
Denounces unjust judges and calls upon God to break the teeth of the wicked and execute swift judgment.
Prayer for deliverance from enemies who lie in wait; God is a defense and a refuge in times of trouble.
A lament after military defeat with a prayer for God's help; through God we shall do valiantly.
A prayer from the end of the earth: lead me to the rock higher than I, and let me abide in thy tabernacle.
My soul waits upon God alone; he is my rock and salvation; trust in him at all times, pour out your heart.
Longing for God in the wilderness: thy lovingkindness is better than life; my soul follows hard after thee.
A prayer for protection from the secret conspiracies of the wicked; God shoots his arrow and they fall.
A harvest thanksgiving praising God for answering prayer, providing water, and crowning the year with goodness.
A communal hymn praising God's awesome deeds, and a personal testimony of prayer answered and vows fulfilled.
A missionary psalm asking God to bless Israel so that all nations may know his saving health.
A triumphant processional celebrating God's march through history, scattering enemies, and dwelling in Zion.
A messianic psalm of deep suffering, zeal for God's house, and imprecations upon persecutors, ending in praise.
A brief, urgent prayer for God to hasten and help, nearly identical to the closing of Psalm 40.
An elderly person's prayer for continued protection and praise, recounting God's faithfulness from youth to old age.
A prayer for the ideal king who brings justice to the poor, peace to the land, and universal dominion.
Asaph wrestles with the prosperity of the wicked until he enters God's sanctuary and understands their end.
A communal lament over the destruction of the sanctuary, pleading with God to remember his covenant and act.
God declares he will judge at the appointed time; the horns of the wicked will be cut off.
Celebrates God's fearsome power in Zion, breaking the weapons of war and rescuing the meek of the earth.
A night of spiritual distress, questioning whether God has forgotten mercy, resolved by remembering his ancient wonders.
A long historical psalm recounting Israel's rebellion from Egypt to the monarchy and God's patient but just responses.
A lament over the destruction of Jerusalem and the defilement of the temple, pleading for mercy and deliverance.
A prayer for restoration using the metaphor of a vine planted and then ravaged; cause thy face to shine.
A festival psalm in which God speaks, urging obedience and lamenting the blessings Israel forfeited by stubbornness.
God stands in the divine council and judges unjust rulers: ye are gods, but ye shall die like men.
A prayer against a confederacy of nations plotting to destroy Israel, invoking God's past victories.
Expresses deep longing for the courts of the Lord; a day in thy courts is better than a thousand elsewhere.
A prayer for national restoration: mercy and truth are met together; righteousness and peace have kissed.
David's personal supplication: bow down thine ear, for I am poor and needy; thou art great and full of compassion.
Celebrates Zion as the city of God where all nations are counted among her citizens.
The darkest psalm: a prayer of unrelieved suffering and isolation, ending with the word 'darkness.'
Celebrates the Davidic covenant and God's faithfulness, then shifts to a lament over the apparent rejection of the anointed.
A prayer of Moses on human frailty and God's eternity: teach us to number our days and apply our hearts to wisdom.
A psalm of divine protection: he that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall not fear terror or plague.
A Sabbath song declaring it is good to praise the Lord; the righteous flourish like the palm tree.
The Lord reigneth, clothed with majesty and strength; his throne is established of old; he is from everlasting.
Calls upon the God of vengeance to judge the proud and arrogant oppressors; blessed is the man God chastens.
A call to worship: come let us sing to the Lord; today if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts.
Sing a new song to the Lord; declare his glory among the nations; he comes to judge the earth in righteousness.
The Lord reigneth with fire and clouds; the earth sees and trembles; light is sown for the righteous.
Sing a new song: the Lord's right hand has done marvelous things; all the earth rejoices before the coming judge.
The Lord reigns in holiness and Zion; Moses, Aaron, and Samuel called upon him and he answered.
Make a joyful noise unto the Lord; know that the Lord he is God; his mercy endures to all generations.
David's pledge to walk in integrity, shun evil, and maintain a faithful household and righteous government.
A prayer of the afflicted: life is brief as grass, but God endures forever and will restore Zion.
Bless the Lord, O my soul: he forgives, heals, redeems, crowns with lovingkindness, and is slow to anger.
A magnificent creation hymn detailing God's wisdom in designing the earth, waters, creatures, and seasons.
Recounts God's covenant faithfulness from Abraham through Joseph and Moses to the giving of the promised land.
A historical confession of Israel's repeated sins from Egypt through the wilderness and Canaan, yet God heard their cry.
Four pictures of people in distress — wanderers, prisoners, the sick, and sailors — all delivered when they cry to the Lord.
A psalm of praise and prayer for victory, combining portions of Psalms 57 and 60 to exalt God among the nations.
An imprecatory psalm against a treacherous accuser, calling for justice, and ending with praise for God's help.
A messianic psalm: the Lord says to my Lord, sit at my right hand; thou art a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.
An acrostic hymn praising God's works, faithfulness, and provision; the fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.
An acrostic psalm describing the blessed man who fears the Lord: gracious, righteous, and generous.
Praise the Lord who is exalted above all nations yet stoops to raise the poor and seat them with princes.
Celebrates the Exodus: when Israel went out of Egypt, the sea fled, Jordan turned back, and mountains skipped.
Not unto us but unto thy name give glory; contrasts the living God with lifeless idols that cannot see or hear.
Personal thanksgiving for deliverance from death; I will pay my vows before all his people.
The shortest chapter in the Bible: a universal call for all nations to praise the Lord for his mercy and truth.
A processional psalm of thanksgiving: the stone which the builders refused is become the head stone of the corner.
The longest chapter in the Bible: a 176-verse acrostic meditation celebrating the beauty and power of God's word.
A song of ascents lamenting deceitful speech and the distress of dwelling among those who hate peace.
A song of ascents: I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills; the Lord is thy keeper and shade.
A song of ascents rejoicing at going to the house of the Lord and praying for the peace of Jerusalem.
A song of ascents: the eyes look to the Lord for mercy amid the contempt of the proud.
A song of ascents: if it had not been the Lord on our side, the waters would have overwhelmed us.
A song of ascents: those who trust in the Lord shall be as Mount Zion, which cannot be removed.
A song of ascents: when the Lord turned again the captivity of Zion, our mouth was filled with laughter; they that sow in tears shall reap in joy.
A song of ascents: except the Lord build the house, they labor in vain; children are a heritage from the Lord.
A song of ascents: blessed is everyone that fears the Lord; thy wife as a fruitful vine, children as olive plants.
A song of ascents: Israel recounts affliction from youth yet has not been overcome; a curse on those who hate Zion.
A song of ascents: out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O Lord; with him is plenteous redemption.
A song of ascents: a brief, tender expression of childlike humility and quiet rest in the Lord.
A song of ascents recounting David's zeal for the ark and God's covenant promise to establish his throne in Zion.
A song of ascents: how good and pleasant for brethren to dwell together in unity, like anointing oil flowing down.
The final song of ascents: a call to bless the Lord in the night watches and a responsive blessing from Zion.
A hymn of praise for God's sovereignty, his mighty acts in history, and the vanity of the nations' idols.
The Great Hallel: every verse ends with the refrain 'for his mercy endureth for ever,' recounting creation and deliverance.
By the rivers of Babylon, the exiles weep and refuse to sing the Lord's song in a strange land.
David praises God wholeheartedly for answered prayer and faithfulness; kings of the earth shall praise the Lord.
A meditation on God's complete knowledge and presence: thou hast searched me and known me; I am fearfully and wonderfully made.
A prayer for deliverance from violent and deceitful men, with confidence in God's justice for the afflicted.
An evening prayer: set a watch before my mouth; keep me from the snares of the wicked.
A prayer from the cave: when my spirit is overwhelmed, thou knowest my path; thou art my refuge and portion.
A penitential prayer: enter not into judgment; teach me to do thy will; quicken me for thy name's sake.
Praise to God as rock and fortress; a prayer for deliverance and national prosperity with happy families.
David's acrostic hymn: great is the Lord and greatly to be praised; his kingdom is everlasting.
Praise the Lord: trust not in mortal princes but in God who keeps truth forever and upholds the oppressed.
Praise God who heals the brokenhearted, counts the stars by name, and gives his word and statutes to Israel.
A cosmic call to praise: angels, sun, moon, stars, sea creatures, mountains, trees, kings, and all people.
Sing a new song with dancing and instruments; the saints execute the written judgment upon the nations.
The final doxology: praise God in his sanctuary with trumpet, psaltery, harp, timbrel, strings, organs, and cymbals; let everything that has breath praise the Lord.

crossReferences.note: Quoted by Paul: Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee.
Quoted in Hebrews regarding Christ's incarnation and exaltation over all things.
Peter quotes this at Pentecost as a prophecy of Christ's resurrection.
Jesus cries from the cross: My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me.
They parted my garments among them and upon my vesture did they cast lots.
The Lord is my shepherd parallels Jesus declaring himself the good shepherd.
He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken — fulfilled at the crucifixion.
Sacrifice and offering thou didst not desire; a body hast thou prepared me.
He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me — Judas' betrayal.
Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever — applied to Christ in Hebrews.
Paul quotes David's confession: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest.
Thou hast ascended on high, thou hast led captivity captive — applied to Christ's ascension.
The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up — applied to Jesus cleansing the temple.
They gave me also gall for my meat and vinegar for my thirst — fulfilled at the crucifixion.
Jesus quotes: I said, ye are gods — in his defense before the Jews.
Satan quotes this psalm during the temptation of Jesus in the wilderness.
Extensively quoted in Hebrews warning against hardening the heart as in the wilderness.
Applied to Christ as creator: Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth.
The most-quoted OT verse in the NT: The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand.
Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek — central to Hebrews' argument.
The stone which the builders refused is become the head stone of the corner.
Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord — shouted at Jesus' triumphal entry.

verses.reason: Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly — a foundational wisdom text.
Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee — a key messianic declaration.
Thou, O Lord, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.
I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, Lord, only makest me dwell in safety.
My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O Lord; in the morning will I direct my prayer.
What is man, that thou art mindful of him? A profound question on human dignity.
Thou hast made him a little lower than the angels and crowned him with glory.
The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God — a widely quoted declaration.
In thy presence is fulness of joy; at thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore.
Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings.
The Lord is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer — a classic declaration of trust.
The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork.
Let the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart, be acceptable in thy sight.
My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me? — Jesus quoted this from the cross.
The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want — perhaps the most memorized verse in the Bible.
Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil.
The earth is the Lord's, and the fulness thereof — a foundational declaration of divine ownership.
The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
One thing have I desired of the Lord, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the Lord.
Weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
Into thine hand I commit my spirit — Jesus' last words from the cross.
Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
O taste and see that the Lord is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.
For with thee is the fountain of life: in thy light shall we see light.
Delight thyself also in the Lord; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
Be still, and know that I am God — one of the most quoted verses in the Bible.
Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
A broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.
Cast thy burden upon the Lord, and he shall sustain thee.
What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee.
Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation.
Because thy lovingkindness is better than life, my lips shall praise thee.
If I regard iniquity in my heart, the Lord will not hear me.
The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up — quoted when Jesus cleansed the temple.
Whom have I in heaven but thee? And there is none upon earth that I desire beside thee.
My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.
I have said, Ye are gods; and all of you are children of the most High — quoted by Jesus in John 10:34.
A day in thy courts is better than a thousand. I had rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God.
Mercy and truth are met together; righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
So teach us to number our days, that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom.
He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your heart — quoted extensively in Hebrews.
Know ye that the Lord he is God: it is he that hath made us, and not we ourselves.
For the Lord is good; his mercy is everlasting; and his truth endureth to all generations.
Bless the Lord, O my soul: and all that is within me, bless his holy name.
As far as the east is from the west, so far hath he removed our transgressions from us.
O give thanks unto the Lord, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand — the most-quoted OT verse in the NT.
Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek — central to the epistle to the Hebrews.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom — echoing the Proverbs.
Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints.
The stone which the builders refused is become the head stone of the corner.
This is the day which the Lord hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.
I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help.
My help cometh from the Lord, which made heaven and earth.
Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: they shall prosper that love thee.
They that sow in tears shall reap in joy.
Except the Lord build the house, they labour in vain that build it.
Lo, children are an heritage of the Lord: and the fruit of the womb is his reward.
Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O Lord — the famous De Profundis.
How good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity!
O give thanks unto the Lord; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yea, we wept, when we remembered Zion.
I am fearfully and wonderfully made: marvellous are thy works.
Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts.
Set a watch, O Lord, before my mouth; keep the door of my lips.
The Lord is nigh unto all them that call upon him, to all that call upon him in truth.
He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.
Let every thing that hath breath praise the Lord. Praise ye the Lord — the grand closing of the Psalter.
